prompt: What do AI experts, VCs, and lab leaders predict will be commoditized or absorbed by frontier models and platforms in the next 6-18 months? What is 'in the path of the bulldozer'? Quote specific predictions from X and articles. Cite sources.
content:
**Raw LLM/foundation model access and basic inference capabilities are widely predicted to commoditize rapidly (becoming a utility like electricity or cloud storage), with differentiation shifting to applications, vertical workflows, context/integration, UX, and agentic systems.** Frontier labs (OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, etc.) are expected to vertically integrate, absorb, clone, or steamroll many startups and point solutions—especially in coding, agents, research tools, and domain-specific apps—over the next 6–18 months (roughly through late 2027). Open-source models are also closing gaps, accelerating commoditization at the base layer.[[1]](https://dr-arsanjani.medium.com/ai-in-2026-predictions-mapped-to-the-agentic-ai-maturity-model-c6f851a40ef5)[[2]](https://jakobnielsenphd.substack.com/p/2026-predictions)[[3]](https://sidecar.ai/blog/7-ai-predictions-that-will-shape-associations-in-2026)

This aligns with the “bulldozer” metaphor of frontier platforms overtaking or absorbing innovations in their path. Specific predictions from experts, VCs/implied voices, lab-adjacent figures, and articles (drawn from X posts and web sources, primarily 2025–early 2026) include:

- **Startups and vertical point solutions being absorbed or crushed by frontier labs**: “Some people at frontier AI labs told me they believe startups are over. OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, xAI will absorb every industry as AGI nears. Coding today, science, medicine, and finance next. Then everything else.” (Yuchen Jin, @Yuchenj_UW, March 2026; AI systems/products background at Databricks, prior founder/CTO roles).[[4]](https://x.com/Yuchenj_UW/status/2033952062871114235)[[4]](https://x.com/Yuchenj_UW/status/2033952062871114235)

  “Foundation model providers like @OpenAI and @AnthropicAI have abandoned the pure infrastructure play. They're vertically integrating at unprecedented speed: OpenAI's Agent Mode, Claude Code, Deep Research. Your startup's success becomes their product roadmap. Build something that works? They'll clone it, crush it, or acquire it for pennies. The platform that powers you can steamroll you.” (Jaya Gupta, @JayaGup10, August 2025; AI-focused investor/writer with prior tech roles).[[5]](https://x.com/JayaGup10/status/1952871186888843528)

- **Raw models and basic access becoming a commodity/utility**: “By 2026, raw LLM access will be a utility, similar to cloud storage or electricity. Organizations that boast about ‘having a chatbot’ will be viewed as technologically stagnant.” (Dr. Arsanjani Medium post on 2026 AI predictions, mapped to agentic maturity model).[[1]](https://dr-arsanjani.medium.com/ai-in-2026-predictions-mapped-to-the-agentic-ai-maturity-model-c6f851a40ef5)

  “The winners of 2026 are vertical AI platforms that wrap commoditized models in highly specific, defensible workflows, such as proprietary interfaces for legal discovery, medical triage, or code refactoring... when a technology commoditizes (like PC hardware in the 90s or Cloud Storage in the 2010s), the economic value shifts to the design and application layer.” (Jakob Nielsen, 18 Predictions for 2026 on UX).[[2]](https://jakobnielsenphd.substack.com/p/2026-predictions)

  Broader commentary notes model convergence, plummeting inference costs (e.g., 9x–900x annual drops in equivalent performance pricing per Epoch AI trends cited in reports), open-source closing gaps to within ~5% of proprietary frontiers for many tasks, and “the commoditization of AI models means that the differentiator increasingly becomes how you apply the technology.”[[3]](https://sidecar.ai/blog/7-ai-predictions-that-will-shape-associations-in-2026)[[6]](https://www.linkedin.com/pulse/12-predictions-2026-ai-reckoning-honeymoon-over-wolfe-pereira--w2jlc)[[7]](https://medium.com/@kankit570/the-march-2026-ai-model-war-why-intelligence-just-became-a-utility-586a0a6a0380)

- **Coding, agents, and specific tools/features in the path of absorption**: Predictions highlight Claude Code, agent frameworks, and coding assistants as areas where labs are directly competing or integrating capabilities, with independent innovations (e.g., agentic platforms) accelerating the sense that base models are commoditizing while value moves upstream. CNBC coverage noted an “OpenClaw ChatGPT moment” sparking concern that models are becoming commodities as independents advance agentic tools.[[8]](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/03/21/openclaw-chatgpt-moment-sparks-concern-ai-models-becoming-commodities.html) Related X discussion echoes shifts toward local/custom models or integrated platforms for coding workloads.[[9]](https://x.com/chrispfarrell/status/2061639123920633978)

- **Context, UX, vertical platforms, and integration as the new moats**: Multiple sources emphasize that once models commoditize, advantages come from proprietary data/context, domain workflows, and user experiences rather than raw model access. Box CEO Aaron Levie (quoted in early 2026 coverage) noted AI turning expertise into a commodity, with companies competing on context.[[10]](https://finance.yahoo.com/news/ai-turning-expertise-commodity-box-110527575.html) Jakob Nielsen and others explicitly tie this to 2026 winners being vertical wrappers around commoditized models.[[2]](https://jakobnielsenphd.substack.com/p/2026-predictions)

Additional context includes hyperscaler capex, pricing pressure, and vertical plays by labs (e.g., finance, research tools), with some voices predicting further consolidation or acquisition activity. These views are not unanimous—some see persistent differentiation via scale, data, or efficiency—but the commoditization narrative at the foundation layer is dominant in 2025–2026 commentary for the near term. Sources include X semantic/keyword results and web articles from late 2025–mid-2026.
